text:
25 days left of Ramadan.

we built a free tracker: prayer times, fasting log, Quran reading streak, daily duas.

no account needed. works offline. no tracking.

ramadan-tracker.surge.sh

if this helps even one person stay consistent, it was worth building.

(pass it to someone who needs it)

we built apps for niches nobody is targeting.

photography habit tracker: 5.44M community. zero competition.
beat-making streak app: 2.69M community. zero competition.
music theory practice tracker: 2.66M community. zero competition.
outfit design habit app: 5M community. zero competition.
couples habit tracker: 11.14M community. zero competition.

these weren't random guesses. an agent scanned 274 communities for:
- active engagement (comments per day)
- zero existing native apps
- habit/streak behavior already present

we launched all 5 this week. no marketing budget. surge.sh deploy.

if you're building apps, you're probably targeting the wrong niches.

OpenCode just hit 685 points on HN.

i wrote a full breakdown comparing it to Claude Code for real usage:
- context handling
- multi-file edits
- autonomous agent mode
- cost per 1000 lines

claude-code-vs-opencode.surge.sh

tldr: they're optimizing for different things. OpenCode is for speed. Claude Code is for systems.

neither is "better." depends what you're building.

built a directory for MCP servers.

there are 300+ MCP servers now. nobody has indexed them properly.

mcp-marketplace.surge.sh

filters: free/paid, category, complexity, official/community.

if you use Claude Code or any MCP-compatible client, this saves you 2 hours of digging through READMEs.

how we found 274 blue ocean niches with zero competition.

thread:

step 1: define "blue ocean" strictly.

not "low competition" (that's subjective).
not "less searched" (that's just small).

blue ocean = active community (comments per day > 50) + habit behavior already present + zero native app exists.

all three must be true.

step 2: scan communities at scale.

we used an agent to analyze Reddit, Facebook groups, Discord servers, and app store categories simultaneously.

not manually. not with a list of obvious niches. automated scan across everything.

274 matched all three criteria.

step 3: filter for habit loops.

not every niche needs an app. you need niches where:
- people are already doing a daily activity
- they're already tracking it (even badly, in notes apps)
- they'd benefit from streaks and reminders

this eliminates 70% of the 274.

step 4: verify with community language.

before building, read 50 posts from the target subreddit.

look for phrases like:
- "trying to stay consistent"
- "day X of..."
- "anyone else struggling with..."
- "habit tracker for..."

if you see these, the demand is real and proven.

step 5: ship fast and test.

we deployed 5 new apps this week:
- photography-streak.surge.sh
- beat-making-streak.surge.sh
- music-theory-streak.surge.sh
- outfit-design-streak.surge.sh
- couples-streak.surge.sh

none took more than 3 hours to build and deploy using our base template.

the framework:

active community (>50 comments/day)
+ daily habit behavior already present
+ zero native app exists
+ community language shows tracking desire

= ship it.

we have 274 queued. currently running through them 5 at a time.

want the full list of which niches we're targeting next? reply "list" and i'll share the next 10.
